content.problemStatement:
Chef has NNN magic spells. The iii-th spell has a volatility of ViV_iVi​ and a strength of AiA_iAi​.

When spells iii and jjj (1≤i<j≤N1 \leq i \lt j \leq N1≤i<j≤N) are activated together, they merge to form a single spell of strength (Ai×Vj+Vi×Aj)(A_i\times V_j + V_i\times A_j)(Ai​×Vj​+Vi​×Aj​).

What's the **maximum** possible strength of a spell Chef can obtain by activating exactly two of his spells?


Input Format

The first line of input will contain a single integer TTT, denoting the number of test cases.
Each test case consists of multiple lines of input.

The first line of each test case contains a single integer NNN, the number of spells.
The next NNN lines describe the spells. The iii-th of them contains two space-separated integers ViV_iVi​ and AiA_iAi​ — the volatility and strength of the iii-th spell.




Output Format
For each test case, output on a new line the maximum possible power of a combined spell.

sampleTestCases.explanation:
Explanation:
Test case 111: There are only two spells. Combining them results in a spell of strength 1×5+5×10=551\times 5 + 5\times 10 = 551×5+5×10=55.
Test case 222: It's optimal to choose the second and third spells. Combining them results in a spell of strength 8×2+10×9=1068\times 2 + 10\times 9 = 1068×2+10×9=106.
Test case 333: It's optimal to choose the first and fourth spells. Combining them results in a spell of strength 45×45+35×35=325045\times 45 + 35\times 35 = 325045×45+35×35=3250.
